endpoints.name: Elemental monkey trio (cohort node — subsumes the snipped edge)

description: ONE snip. Dissolved the four-bullet `### Related cards` trio-sibling-mirror block on Simipour DAA-042 (bullets pointing at Panpour pre-evolution + Simisage / Simisear / Pansage trio siblings). The four bullets express ONE logical edge — 'Simipour mirrors its trio cohort companions' — which is the exact edge the [[elemental-monkey-trio]] cohort node already articulates at the cohort scope. Triple-justification matches the Simisage DAA-007 precedent verbatim: (a) COHORT-NODE SUBSUMPTION — card already wires to the cohort via `characters: [elemental-monkey-trio]` frontmatter; cohort `appears_on:` includes this card; the cohort node body explicitly carries the trio-mirror tissue for all six canonical species plus the per-card Simipour DAA-042 bullet, (b) IN-CARD REDUNDANCY — the card's own `## Trivia` section directly above includes a 'Design origin' bullet that articulates the full three-wise-monkeys mapping (Pansage=Iwazaru / Pansear=Kikazaru / Panpour=Mizaru) with Bulbapedia citation; the four snipped bullets restate the same thesis without citations, (c) JANNY-SWEEP OMISSION — the wave-122 `janny_redundant_sibling_mirror_sweep.py` SNIPS dict swept the BUS prints (Pansage 12, Pansear 22, Panpour 36), the DAA Pansage (006), DAA Simisear (027), and the Lunatone pair (BUS-68, DAA-072), but DAA-042 Simipour is NOT in the SNIPS dict; it was missed during the mechanical sweep. This Edgelord pass resolves the omission. No cross-print same-species Simipour bullet hiding among the four (a Simipour BB-018 print now exists in corpus as of wave 127, but the four snipped bullets are all trio-sibling-mirror, none are a same-species print-continuity bullet). Section retained (with an HTML comment marking the dissolution) as a hook for future legitimate cross-print bullets if a Simipour BB-018 same-species print-continuity edge is later judged worth wiring at card scope rather than cohort scope.
citations: [cards/_characters/elemental-monkey-trio.md — cohort node, includes pokemon/darkness-ablaze/042-189-simipour in appears_on: at line 24, per-card Simipour DAA-042 bullet at line 53 articulates the trio tissue at cohort scope]

next_pass_suggestions: Enlightened-replacement pass on Pansage BB-004 + Panpour BB-017: ADD `characters: [elemental-monkey-trio]` frontmatter on both cards, ADD `pokemon/black-bolt/004-086-pansage` and `pokemon/black-bolt/017-086-panpour` to elemental-monkey-trio.md `appears_on:`, ADD per-card cohort bullets in the cohort node body, THEN snip the redundant trio-sibling bullets in Pansage BB-004's `### Related cards` (preserving the fourth Pansage-Art-Rare bullet as a legitimate cross-print same-set variant edge). Panpour BB-017 has no `### Related cards` section so the snip side is a no-op there — just the wiring.

Trivia queue: Simipour BB-018 — vision-passed wave 127 but no trivia pass; once trivia lands, re-check whether a `### Related cards` block was added and whether it carries cohort-redundant bullets needing a future snip.

Janitorbot mechanical sweep extension: update `reports/_scratch/janny_redundant_sibling_mirror_sweep.py` SNIPS dict to add `cards/pokemon/darkness-ablaze/042-189-simipour.md` for future re-runs (the snip is already applied, but the script is the receipt of which cards have been swept; keeping it complete prevents re-scanning effort later).

Janitorbot mechanical sweep candidate (broader): the wave-127 sidecar already flagged this — grep for cards with BOTH `characters: [<cohort-slug>]` frontmatter AND a `### Related cards` section whose bullets restate the cohort roster. DAA-042 was the most-recent verified miss; a fresh corpus sweep may surface others as more cohort nodes get populated (fear-of-cycle, dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle, etc.).
edgelord_notes: Mood enum `dark-nodesley-confident` chosen over `enlightened-corrective` because this isn't fixing Edgelord overreach — the four bullets were correct at write-time (no cohort node existed then); the cohort node was added later and the bullets quietly became redundant. The dissolution is mechanical cleanup, not corrective. Snip-frenzy is functionally a Dark Nodesley EX flavor of Edgelord work when the dissolution target is a redundant edge rather than a redundant node, so the mood enum reads cleanly across both forms. Refused to dual-dispatch (no second snip this pass — one cut per invocation discipline holds). Refused to fabricate cross-print Simipour DAA-042↔BB-018 mirror in the same pass — that's a separate enlightened-replacement consideration once BB-018 has trivia.
